discussion_comments.comment_body: It's not ideal to have time based waits in tests, can you use an event based wait?

We need more test cases here. 

And the behavior below is flaky. The format mentioned in the docs is not tested here. 
So we should make it strict, then test and document it well. 

`dateRange 13:00, 14:00` fails
`dateRange  13:00, 14:00` passes (two spaces)
`dateRange: 13:00, 14:00` fails
`dateRange : 13:00, 14:00` passes

I've made it more strict by removing the logic that would attempt to parse the date using `new Date` after `dayjs.isValid()` returns `false` (see [this commit](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/pull/5220/commits/81b57d6b2276f1200d5638e181165e2bd7648a62)). This change has not broken any tests, but I suspect it might break existing charts that are passing an invalid `dateFormat` while still successfully rendering due to that "backup" logic.

For valid date ranges, I've now included a test with a date-only range, a time only range, and a date+time range. Are there any other test cases I should add?

For the tests for invalid date ranges, I realised that depending on the specific error in syntax, the `dateRange` declaration could either be ignored or throw an error.

From your examples above, `dateRange: 13:00, 14:00` would be ignored since the `dateRange:` (without a space before the colon) would not be considered a match by the jison parser looking for `"dateRange"\s[^#\n;]`, and the `dateRange` would remain as the default of `''`. For `dateRange : 13:00, 14:00` (space before colon), the declaration would be picked up, but an error will be thrown as `: 13:00` would be considered an invalid date. I've added tests for both these scenarios (see [this commit](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/pull/5220/commits/0444adb25c7100cb9ae3667b440cd8caa7c8258e)), and would appreciate any feedback!

Please add more test cases that handle dates as well, currently only time is used. 
Then some cases where there are tasks before and after the given range.

I've started, but am new to cypress and having difficulties getting the tests to pass locally / produce screenshots that look right. Will keep going at it over the next few days and post in the Discord if I have any specific questions!

These tests are flaky:

![Flowchart-Icon-2-icon-verify-if-icons-are-working-from-fontawesome-library-forest-theme diff](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/34fc0da3-578f-4b24-811a-98022ebfd729)

I think we need to:

- Instead of using https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/2fb7bc2806f3d91dc9840ae988dc06e1329ca9a9/cypress/platform/e2e.html#L9-L12, we might want to use a local version of FontAwesome, like what the docs site uses: https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/blob/3eaf8b717770a119cd0ea5303632b6430330f239/packages/mermaid/src/docs/package.json#L21
- We might need something like `await Promise.all(Array.from(document.fonts, (font) => font.load()))` to forcibly wait for all fonts to load before starting to render the diagram: https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli/blob/7b19bf7f230b95fde1ba1a4bbda78bc96e696773/src/index.js#L255

Can you add the `imgSnapshotTest`s, for the other scenarios of dateRange?

- Actual date
- Only start
- Only end

Certainly, but how do these work?  Where are the images produced so that I can check them?  I suspect that without altering the tasks, the display is going to be messed up, with the task bars invading the margins, but I don't know how to produce any diagram output from this repo.

To verify snapshots:

```sh
pnpm e2e
open cypress/snapshots
```

Can you also add this into the contribution docs if it's not present there?

Love the tests!
Can you please revert the changes to the titles of existing tests, and wrap all the new tests in a `describe` block instead of editing the title?

Otherwise, our image comparison tests will not be happy with the changed titles.
